seo.title: Verified Domains on {{explorerName}}
seo.description: Learn what verified domains are on {{explorerName}}, how to set a domain, and how to verify ownership.

images.accountPage: Verified domains on your account page

intro.proof: Domain verification proves that the same entity controls a particular {{explorerName}} address and domain.
intro.transparency: Verifying your domain improves transparency and helps establish you as a trusted {{explorerName}} service operator.
intro.twoWay: Anyone can set any domain for their {{explorerName}} address. Verification therefore requires a two-way link between the domain operator and the address.
intro.domainClaim: The domain must claim ownership of the address.
intro.addressClaim: The address must claim ownership by the domain.

website.title: Verified domains on our website
website.listBefore: On our website you can view
website.examples: Examples of accounts with verified domains:
website.badge: Bithomp’s {{ledgerName}} explorer displays a verification badge next to the avatar. The domain appears below it as a clickable green link with a checkmark.
website.reverify: Our system re-verifies every verified domain every 24 hours, so the checkmark is shown only for addresses and domains that still link to each other.
website.recommendation: We recommend uploading and validating the TOML file before setting the domain on your {{explorerName}} account. In this order, the domain usually appears as verified within a minute; otherwise verification can take up to 24 hours.
